region_of_origin: Temperate regions worldwide; commonly consumed in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers pear (whole); green variety is a colour variant within this class.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium green pear (approximately 150 g) is a typical single serving for an adult.

nutrient_source.title: Pear, raw — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database for fresh pear (green variety). USDA FoodData Central NDB 09252 (Pears, raw) used to cross-validate and supplement micronutrient fields not reported by HPB.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 38 (low) for fresh pear is reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 15.2 × 1.5) / 100 = 8.7 per 150 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Zero sodium, 130 mg potassium per 100 g, and 3.0 g dietary fibre
hypertension.guidance: Green pear is sodium-free and provides 130 mg potassium per 100 g, supporting vascular tone; its 3.0 g dietary fibre further contributes to blood pressure management, making it a suitable daily fruit choice for people with hypertension when consumed as whole fruit rather than juice.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI of 38 and 3.0 g dietary fibre moderating glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Green pear has a low glycemic index of 38 and 3.0 g fibre per 100 g, which slows glucose absorption; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may include one medium pear (≈150 g) per serving while monitoring total carbohydrate intake and preferring whole fruit over juice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, 3.0 g soluble and insoluble fibre, and flavonoid polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Green pear contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, provides 3.0 g dietary fibre per 100 g that supports LDL reduction, and delivers quercetin and other flavonoids in the skin that have anti-inflammatory and endothelial-protective properties, making it a heart-healthy fruit for daily consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fibre and quercetin present but no direct omega-3 or strong anti-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Green pear provides 3.0 g fibre and quercetin polyphenols in the skin that support gut microbiome health and mild anti-inflammatory pathways; however, it lacks omega-3 fatty acids and other potent anti-inflammatory nutrients, so it is best consumed as part of a broader anti-inflammatory dietary pattern rather than relied upon as a primary RA management food.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Green pear (Pear, green) is a fresh, raw temperate fruit with a light green skin, crisp juicy flesh, and a mild sweet flavour. It is consumed whole and uncooked, typically as a snack or dessert fruit. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 66 kcal, 15.2 g carbohydrate (of which 12.4 g is natural fruit sugar), 3.0 g dietary fibre, 0.4 g protein, and negligible fat. The fibre content — both soluble pectin and insoluble fibre — slows glucose absorption and supports gut health. Green pear has a low glycemic index of approximately 38, making it suitable for people managing Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions. It is sodium-free and provides 130 mg potassium per 100 g, which is beneficial for blood pressure management in hypertension. The skin contains quercetin and other flavonoid polyphenols with antioxidant and mild anti-inflammatory properties relevant to cardiovascular and joint health. Green pear is naturally vegan, gluten-free, halal, and kosher. It is widely available in Singapore and consumed as a fresh snack, in fruit salads, or alongside savoury dishes. It is known as 绿梨 in Chinese, Pear hijau in Malay, பச்சை பேரிக்காய் in Tamil, and हरा नाशपाती in Hindi.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (confidence 0.95 per input record). GI value of 38 confirmed via University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed publication. USDA FoodData Central NDB 09252 used to cross-validate and supplement micronutrient fields. All values are consistent across sources.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database for fresh green pear; pears are naturally a negligible-fat fruit (confirmed zero). saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all 0.0 as pears contain no meaningful fat fractions (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central NDB 09252). iron_mg is 0.0 per HPB source; confirmed zero at the reported precision level. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as this is a plant food. vitamin_b12_mcg and vitamin_d_mcg are 0.0 as these are absent in plant foods (naturally zero). Micronutrient values for vitamin_c_mg (4.3), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (1.0), B-vitamins, phosphorus_mg (12.0), magnesium_mg (7.0), zinc_mg (0.1), selenium_mcg (0.1), and water_g (83.7) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central NDB 09252 (Pears, raw) as HPB does not report these fields; values are consistent with the HPB primary data. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (38 × 15.2 g carbohydrate × 1.5 serving size factor) / 100 = 8.7. The rheumatoid_arthritis health context from the input record has been preserved and corrected: the input contained an internal contradiction (suitable: false but reason described positive factors); the output sets concern_level to moderate with a balanced clinical note. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 and SNOMED CT code 72807008 are defensible matches for pear (whole); FoodEx2 code A00LQ (Pears) is the standard EFSA FoodEx2 category.
